mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, consumer spending power contracts and recession fears dominate market pricing, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during post-2010 expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable consumer prices indicate robust demand without inflationary pressure, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 5% annually while CPIAUCSL remains flat, or interaction term IC < 0.01 over 24 months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals immediate capacity utilization improvements and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during periods of quantitative tightening, or the signal reverses direction when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and corporate revenue forecasts, producing consistently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day SPX returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year housing starts decline exceeding 10% signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and related supply chains, dragging down equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 70% of quarters following a >10% YoY HOUST drop, or the threshold fails to separate bull and bear market outcomes.

mechanism_narrative: Falling federal funds rates alongside declining unemployment indicate a soft landing scenario where monetary easing supports growth without triggering recession, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns when FEDFUNDS falls while UNRATE rises, or the interaction effect vanishes when GDP growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms sustained economic expansion and corporate profit growth, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3% annually, or the signal loses predictive power during financial stress episodes.
